comments.author: @WorldsinStone
comments.text:
While monks were building monasteries on top of 300-meter rock pillars in Greece, on the other side of the world, a tiny town of 1,000 people was carving an entire city into the walls of a canyon — suspended between cliffs, with waterfalls as neighbors and avalanches as a daily reality.
That's Ouray. And the story is just as impossible.
👉 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7bk5xTPAyyQ

comments.author: @withSylviaMonique
comments.text: I have been there with my parents when I was around 10 years old...still remember this huge cooking pan and the hanging in the air bridges from one cliff to another...magical experience....

replies.author: @WorldsinStone
replies.text: Thank you for sharing such a wonderful memory! It’s amazing how places like Meteora can stay with us for a lifetime.

comments.author: @MrDomo66
comments.text: Tintin et le mystère de la toison d'or.😁

replies.author: @WorldsinStone
replies.text: Great reference! 😄 The landscapes and sense of adventure around Meteora really do feel like something straight out of a Tintin story.
